leads.name: University of Pittsburgh Press
leads.notes: AUPresses member. Pittsburgh, PA -- 3.5 hours from Ann Arbor. 15-20 titles/yr. Pitt Poetry Series (50+ years). Humanities, Latinx studies, Slavic studies. Institutional budget. Direct contact: Amy Sherman, Acquisitions Editor (verified on staff page).
leads.pitch: AUPresses institutional publisher in Pittsburgh -- 3.5 hours from Ann Arbor -- with 15 to 20 titles per year including the celebrated Pitt Poetry Series, one of the most respected poetry publishing programs in the country.

leads.name: Purdue University Press
leads.notes: AUPresses member. West Lafayette, IN -- 2 hours from Ann Arbor. 15-20 titles/yr. Technology studies, humanities, Purdue Studies in Romance Literatures. Direct contact: Andrea Gapsch, Acquisitions Editor (verified on staff page). Director Justin Race at racej@purdue.edu.
leads.pitch: AUPresses institutional publisher in West Lafayette, Indiana -- just 2 hours from Ann Arbor -- producing 15 to 20 titles per year across technology studies, humanities, and Purdue Studies in Romance Literatures.

leads.name: Four Way Books
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press, NYC. Founded 1991. ~10 titles/yr of poetry and literary fiction. Active competition program (Intro Prize, Levis Prize). Publisher Martha Rhodes. Verified editors@fourwaybooks.com on contact page (previous four_way_books@fourwaybooks.com bounced).
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press in New York since 1991 -- ~10 titles per year of poetry and literary fiction, with an active competition program that creates a predictable annual production cycle.

leads.name: University of Akron Press
leads.notes: AUPresses member. Akron, OH -- 60 miles from Ann Arbor. ~12-15 titles/yr. Akron Poetry Prize, Ohio/Midwest history, technology studies. Direct contact: Dr. Jon Miller, Director (verified). Sr. Editorial Manager Amy Freels at afreels@uakron.edu.
leads.pitch: AUPresses institutional publisher based in Akron, Ohio -- 60 miles from Ann Arbor -- with 12 to 15 titles per year including the Akron Poetry Prize series, Ohio and Midwest history, and technology studies.

leads.name: Northern Illinois University Press
leads.notes: Now a Cornell University Press imprint since July 2019. ~15-20 titles/yr across political science, history, Southeast Asia studies. Acquisitions handled by Cornell UP. Old niupress@niu.edu bounced. Contact: cornellup_acq@cornell.edu and reference NIU Press imprint.
leads.pitch: Northern Illinois University Press imprint of Cornell University Press -- 15 to 20 titles per year across political science, history, and Southeast Asia studies, with Cornell's institutional backing and distribution infrastructure.

leads.name: Coffee House Press
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press founded 1984. Minneapolis, MN. ~15-20 titles/yr. NBA, NBCC, and PEN Award winners. Distributed by Consortium. Direct contact: Linda Ewing, Executive Director (verified on staff page).
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press since 1984 in Minneapolis -- 15 to 20 titles per year of poetry, fiction, and essays, with National Book Award and PEN Award winners throughout the catalog.

leads.name: Autumn House Press
leads.notes: Independent nonprofit literary press in Pittsburgh, PA. Founded 1998. 6-8 titles/yr of poetry, fiction, and nonfiction. Three annual prize competitions (Poetry, Fiction, Nonfiction). About 4 hours from Ann Arbor. Publisher: Mike Good. NEA-funded. Active and acquiring as of 2026. Replaces CSU Poetry Center which is closing June 2026.
leads.pitch: Independent nonprofit literary press in Pittsburgh, PA since 1998 -- 6 to 8 titles per year of poetry, fiction, and nonfiction, with three active prize competitions about 4 hours from Ann Arbor.

leads.name: New Rivers Press
leads.notes: Literary press at Minnesota State University Moorhead. Founded 1968. ~8-10 titles/yr. Poetry and fiction. American Fiction Prize. 55+ year track record. CLMP member. Senior Editor Kevin Carollo. Email nrp@mnstate.edu confirmed current.
leads.pitch: Literary press at Minnesota State Moorhead, founded 1968 -- one of the oldest continuously operating literary presses in the country at 55+ years, publishing 8 to 10 titles per year of poetry and fiction.

leads.name: Tupelo Press
leads.notes: Nonprofit literary press in North Adams, MA. Founded 2000. ~10-12 titles/yr of poetry, fiction, and nonfiction. Publisher Jeffrey Levine. Multiple active prize competitions (Donald Hall Prize in Poetry, Dorset Prize, Snowbound Series). NEA-funded. 25-year track record. Replaces Ahsahta Press which closed permanently in 2019.
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press in North Adams, MA since 2000 -- 10 to 12 titles per year of poetry, fiction, and nonfiction, with multiple prize programs and NEA funding sustained over a 25-year track record.

leads.name: Ugly Duckling Presse
leads.notes: Nonprofit artists' book and poetry press, Brooklyn, NY. Founded 2001. ~8-12 titles/yr. Translations, poetry, experimental prose. CLMP. Collective model (no single acquisitions editor). Email corrected from info@ to office@uglyducklingpresse.org (verified on contact page).
leads.pitch: Nonprofit artists' book and poetry press in Brooklyn since 2001 -- 8 to 12 titles per year of poetry, translations, and experimental prose, with a distinctive mix of handmade and offset editions.

leads.name: Etruscan Press
leads.notes: Independent literary press affiliated with Wilkes University, Wilkes-Barre, PA. Founded 2001. ~8-10 titles/yr. Poetry, fiction, literary nonfiction. National distribution. Direct contact: Amanda Rabaduex, Managing Editor (verified on editors page). Previous info@ bounced.
leads.pitch: Independent literary press in Wilkes-Barre, PA since 2001 -- 8 to 10 titles per year of poetry, fiction, and literary nonfiction with strong award recognition and national distribution.

leads.name: Acre Books
leads.notes: Literary press at University of Cincinnati. Cincinnati, OH -- ~3 hours from Ann Arbor. Founded 2016. ~6-8 titles/yr. Literary fiction and poetry. Distributed by University of Chicago Press. Publisher Nicola Mason. Email acre.books@uc.edu confirmed correct on website.
leads.pitch: University of Cincinnati-housed literary press since 2016 -- 6 to 8 titles per year of literary fiction and poetry from Cincinnati, Ohio, about 3 hours from Ann Arbor.

leads.name: Hub City Press
leads.notes: Independent nonprofit literary press and bookshop in Spartanburg, SC. Founded 1995. 8-10 titles/yr of literary fiction and nonfiction. Executive Director Betsy Teter. Community-rooted with attached Hub City Bookshop. NEA-funded. Active and acquiring as of 2026. Replaces Tilbury House which was acquired by Penguin Random House (via Cherry Lake) in late 2025.
leads.pitch: Independent nonprofit literary press and bookshop in Spartanburg, SC since 1995 -- 8 to 10 titles per year of literary fiction and nonfiction, with a community bookshop that puts every book directly in front of local readers.

leads.name: Deep Vellum Publishing
leads.notes: Dallas, TX-based literary press founded 2013. ~15-20 titles/yr specializing in world literature in translation. Distributed by Consortium. Acquired Open Letter Books and Dalkey Archive in 2025 -- now one of largest independent translation publishers in US. Direct contact: Will Evans, Publisher & CEO (format confirmed as first@deepvellum.org).
leads.pitch: Dallas-based literary press founded 2013 -- 15 to 20 titles per year specializing in world literature in translation, distributed by Consortium, and recently expanded by acquiring Open Letter Books and Dalkey Archive.

leads.name: Fence Books
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry and fiction press, Albany/NYC. Founded 2001. ~5-8 titles/yr. Associated with Fence magazine. Experimental and challenging work. Editorial Director Emily Wallis Hughes. Correct contact fence.fencebooks@gmail.com (previous info@fenceportal.org unverified).
leads.pitch: Nonprofit poetry and fiction press since 2001 -- 5 to 8 titles per year of experimental and challenging literary work, associated with Fence magazine and operating the National Intro Prize.

leads.name: Pleiades Press
leads.notes: University of Central Missouri literary press and journal. Warrensburg, MO. ~4-6 titles/yr. Long-running literary journal alongside book program. Direct contact: Jenny Molberg, Editor (UCM English faculty -- verified). Also uses pleiadespoetryeditor@gmail.com.
leads.pitch: University of Central Missouri-based literary press and journal -- 4 to 6 book titles per year of poetry and fiction, alongside a long-running literary journal that gives the press national visibility.

leads.name: Word Works
leads.notes: Nonprofit poetry publisher, Washington DC. Founded 1974. ~4-6 titles/yr. Washington Prize, Tenth Gate Prize. 50-year track record. Co-Editors Karren Alenier and Nancy White. Email editor@wordworksbooks.org confirmed current. Series-specific contacts also available (Andrea Carter Brown, Kasey Jueds, etc.).
leads.pitch: Nonprofit poetry publisher in Washington DC since 1974 -- 4 to 6 titles per year including the Washington Prize and Tenth Gate Prize, with a 50-year track record of supporting American poetry.

leads.name: Sundress Publications
leads.notes: Volunteer-run 501(c)(3) nonprofit literary press, Knoxville, TN. ~6-10 titles/yr. Poetry and short fiction. Best of the Net Anthology. Direct contact: Erin Elizabeth Smith, Executive Director (verified on staff page).
leads.pitch: Nonprofit literary press in Knoxville, TN -- 6 to 10 titles per year of poetry and short fiction including chapbooks and full collections, with a growing national profile and the Best of the Net Anthology.

leads.name: Moon City Press
leads.notes: Missouri State University literary press. Springfield, MO. ~4-6 titles/yr. Poetry and fiction. Moon City Poetry Award active 2026. Direct contact: Michael Czyzniejewski, Editor-in-Chief (MSU English Associate Professor -- verified).
leads.pitch: Missouri State University literary press in Springfield, MO -- 4 to 6 titles per year of poetry and fiction, with the Moon City Poetry Award creating a reliable annual production cycle.

leads.name: Conduit Books & Ephemera
leads.notes: Minneapolis-based small literary press. ~3-5 titles/yr. Poetry and experimental work. Associated with Conduit magazine. Editor-in-Chief William Waltz. Domain changed from conduitbooks.org to conduit.org -- email updated accordingly. Active prize competitions (Minds on Fire, Marystina Santiestevan).
leads.pitch: Minneapolis-based literary press -- 3 to 5 titles per year of poetry and experimental work, associated with Conduit magazine and known for distinctive physical design and highly curated selections.
